About me

Press room expertise
meets digital initiative

I am Filip Claeys — teamlead in the press room at Smurfit Westrock Ghent, with a Bachelor's degree in Printing Technology and years of hands-on experience in offset colour management.

Working daily with ISO 12647-2:2013, dot gain curves, spectrophotometers and RIP systems gave me a deep understanding of what press operators actually need — and what the existing tools were missing.

During my personal free time, using only my own laptop running Ubuntu Linux, I built two interactive browser-based tools to digitise and automate the fingerprint and TVI measurement workflows that our press room relied on static spreadsheets for.

This project was built with Claude AI as a coding partner — I provided the domain knowledge, ISO expertise and press room insight; the AI translated that into working code.

  • 🎓
    Bachelor in Printing Technology Formal education in the printing industry
  • 🏭
    Teamlead · Press Room Smurfit Westrock Ghent
  • 📐
    ISO 12647-2:2013 specialist GC2 board coated · Curves A, B, E · AM & FM screening
  • 🎨
    ECG Extended Colour Gamut CMYK + Violet (FM) + Orange + Green
  • 🔬
    X-Rite eXact · DataCatcher Spectrophotometric measurement & direct USB integration
  • Personal Ubuntu Linux laptop No company IT · No Microsoft *!# · Pure initiative
The project

Two browser tools —
no installation required

Single HTML files. Open in any browser. Works offline. Save sessions as .json. Direct input from X-Rite eXact DataCatcher via USB.

📋
Tool 1 · Fingerprint
Fingerprint Tone Value ECG
Fingerprint_Anicolor_ECG.html

Digital replacement of the static ODS fingerprint spreadsheet. Enter measured dot% from 5 sheets × 7 inks × 10 tone steps. Live TVI, averages, standard deviation and ISO compliance per patch.

  • 5 sheets × 7 ink channels × 10 tone steps
  • Murray-Davies TVI — live as you type
  • ISO TV targets: AM (Curve A) and FM/Violet (Curve E)
  • Solid density + LAB colour values with ΔE00
  • TVI charts vs ISO reference per channel
  • Direct X-Rite eXact DataCatcher input
  • Save / load sessions · Export CSV
📊
Tool 2 · TVI Manager
TVI Manager ISO 12647
TVI_Manager_ISO12647.html

Full TVI measurement and compliance management tool. Enter measured dot%, get instant ISO 12647-2:2013 compliance checking, RIP compensation curves and professional PDF reports.

  • CMYK + ECG (CMYKVOG) — 7 ink channels
  • ISO Curves A (CMY/O/G), B (K), E (Violet FM)
  • AM tolerance ±4% · FM tolerance ±5%
  • RIP compensation — ISO Curve A targeted (binary search inversion)
  • Export: 256-pt curve data for Harlequin, Fiery, Prinergy, GMG
  • Multi-session management · save as .json
  • PDF report via Python + ReportLab
Technical accuracy

ISO 12647-2:2013 — fully implemented

All three reference curves with correct tolerances. Violet (FM) correctly uses Curve E — not the AM curve.

Ink channel Screening ISO curve 25% 40% 50% 75% 80% Tolerance
Cyan AMCurve A +11%+14%+15% +14%+12% ±4%
Magenta AMCurve A +11%+14%+15% +14%+12% ±4%
Yellow AMCurve A +11%+14%+15% +14%+12% ±4%
Black AMCurve B +14%+18%+19% +17%+14% ±4%
Violet FMCurve E +20% +26% +26% +20% +16% ±5% FM
Orange AMCurve A +11%+14%+15% +14%+12% ±4%
Green AMCurve A +11%+14%+15% +14%+12% ±4%
Key features

What makes these tools different

📡

X-Rite DataCatcher ready

Input fields accept direct tab/enter output from X-Rite eXact via USB DataCatcher. No re-typing, no paper, one step from measurement to data.

🎨

Live colour-coded compliance

Every ISO check patch colour-coded in real time — green within tolerance, amber marginal, red fail. Operator sees it instantly, before signing off.

⚙️

RIP compensation curves

Plate% calculated by binary-search inversion of the measured press curve, targeting ISO Curve A — not zero gain. Green = reduce, red = increase.

🔮

Violet FM correctly handled

ECG Violet uses FM screening with ISO Curve E (+26% TVI at 40/50%) and wider ±5% tolerance — not mistakenly treated as AM Curve A.

📄

PDF report generator

Professional multi-page A4 PDF via Python + ReportLab: verdict banner, TVI charts, compliance tables, full patch data, RIP compensation curves.

💾

Session persistence

Every press run saved as a dated .json file. Auto-saved to localStorage. Export CSV. Full audit trail across multiple shifts and jobs.

Built with
HTML5 + JavaScript Chart.js Python + ReportLab Murray-Davies TVI ISO 12647-2:2013 X-Rite eXact localStorage + .json Claude AI (Anthropic)

Personal laptop · Ubuntu Linux

Built entirely outside company infrastructure. No company IT, no corporate network, no VPN, no admin rights needed. 100% personal initiative & personal time.

No Microsoft *!# 😄

Get the tools

Available upon request

The HTML tools are not distributed publicly — this ensures they are used correctly for the right substrate, screening method and ISO standard version.

Send a brief description of your press environment — substrate, ink set, RIP system — and I will get back to you.
